title: SSA put()-no-key drops co-owned keys on update
description: put() with key=None uses Patch::Apply (SSA) with field manager 'secretx-k8s'. SSA drops any key previously applied by the same manager that is absent from the new body. If a caller first put()s 'value' key, then put()s 'other-key' via ?key= (merge patch, different manager), then put()s again with no key — SSA will drop 'value' but 'other-key' survives because a different manager owns it. However if a caller does two no-key put()s on different K8sBackend instances pointing at the same Secret, SSA will replace the previously-applied 'value' key. The real danger: any two put()-no-key calls for the same Secret via different field managers would conflict. But 'secretx-k8s' is a hardcoded manager name, so two processes using secretx-k8s on the same Secret will fight via SSA. This is undocumented and will produce data loss.
